title: Andropogon agenium
taxonomy: Andropogon agenium is classified within the family Poaceae, which is commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and is a part of the genus Andropogon. The genus Andropogon is further divided into several species, with Andropogon agenium being one of them. This classification indicates that Andropogon agenium is a type of grass that is closely related to other species in the same genus and family. The exact classification of Andropogon agenium is still subject to ongoing research and may be refined as more information becomes available.
uses: Andropogon agenium, also known as bluestem, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves have been employed to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. intro:
Andropogon agnus-castus, also known as Andropogon agnus-castus, is not the correct scientific name for the plant you are referring to. Andropogon agnus-castus is actually the scientific name of the plant 'Vetiver', a different plant species. The plant you are referring to is likely Andropogon agnium, which is commonly known as 'Blonde psoralea', 'Jointed Witch Grass', or ' Jointed Witchgrass' or 'Blonde psoralea'. 

The plant Andropogon agnium is a type of grass that is native to North America, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of infections, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Andropogon agnium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Andropogon agnium is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections.
